content.full_text:
February 13, 1992

Legal Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Greetings:

Antioch City Council member Cathryn Freitas has authorized and requested me to seek the FPPC's opinion on this matter. A copy of your response may be sent to Mrs. Freitas at P.O. Box 1161, Antioch, CA 94509; however, I would like to receive either the original or a copy of your response.

Mrs. Freitas was a candidate for City Council at the November, 1990 municipal election.

Another governmental agency -- a water district -- sells water to a number of cities in eastern Contra Costa County, including Antioch. The water district is also proposing a large reservoir project in the eastern Contra Costa County area. As part of the reservoir project, the water district has been involved in a number of public education campaigns.

Apparently as part of its interest in keeping in touch with the political pulse of the area, the water district, without Mrs. Freitas' knowledge, commissioned a poll of voters regarding the elections for the Antioch and Brentwood city council races in the November, 1990 elections. An employee of the water district telephoned Mrs. Freitas to convey the results of the District's poll. She indicated that she did not believe in polls and terminated the conversation. Mrs. Freitas does not know whether the district conveyed the poll results to other candidates. She did not see or receive a copy of the poll, nor did she use the poll results in any way to alter the conduct of her campaign.

Legal Division  
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
February 13, 1992  
Page two

Mrs. Freitas desires your opinion on whether she was required to disclose this unauthorized, unsolicited poll and telephone conversation as a "gift" on her campaign disclosure form.

Very truly yours,

William R. Galstan  
City Attorney

WRG/sh  
cc: Council Member Cathryn Freitas
